perturbation.original_text: The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 6 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by changing the automatic renewal period of the Maintenance Term from 12 months to 6 months. This introduces uncertainty as to the actual duration of the renewal terms, leading to potential disputes over the length of the service period and associated fees. The conflict arises specifically with the earlier statement saying 'Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Maintenance Services No-charge Maintenance Services shall be provided from the Delivery Date through the Warranty Period. The first paid (Initial) Maintenance Term shall commence upon expiration of the Warranty Period and shall continue for twelve (12) months thereafter. Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 6 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. During the initial Maintenance Term and any renewal term, Maintenance Fees shall be paid in the increments described below under "Payment Terms."
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal period of the Maintenance Term from 12 months to 6 months. The contradicted text contains both statements, establishing a clear conflict. The modified text directly contradicts the earlier 12-month renewal period stated within the contradicted text. This inconsistency about the renewal period creates a direct contradiction regarding which term length is valid.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be twenty- four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, including Bank of America holidays.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be eight (8) hours per day, five (5) days per week, excluding Bank of America holidays.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the duration of the maintenance period from 24/7 including holidays, to 8/5 excluding holidays. This will cause confusion on the exact time availability on maintenance services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be eight (8) hours per day, five (5) days per week, excluding Bank of America holidays. 120 days METHOD OF DELIVERY AND STATE WHERE SOFTWARE AND DOCUMENTATION ARE RECEIVED The method of delivery and name of the State where Supplier shall deliver and Bank of America shall receive Software and Documentation: TBD TRAINING
perturbation.justification: The original text states the maintenance period is 24/7 including Bank of America holidays. The changed text modifies this to 8 hours a day, 5 days a week, excluding Bank of America holidays. This directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text, which states the maintenance period is 8 hours a day, 5 days a week, excluding Bank of America holidays, thus creating a conflicting and inconsistent obligation.
